steps.title: The minimum of a parabola appears in vertex form
steps.content: For $f(x) = a(x-h)^2 + k$ with $a > 0$, the minimum value is $k$ (a constant). We need the form that shows this $k$ explicitly.

steps.title: Convert $f(x) = x^2 - 4x - 320$ to vertex form
steps.content: Complete the square: $x^2 - 4x = (x-2)^2 - 4$, so $$f(x) = (x-2)^2 - 4 - 320 = (x-2)^2 - 324$$ Minimum value is $-324$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A) $x^2 - 4(x+80)$ — expands to $x^2 - 4x - 320$, but the minimum is not shown as a standalone constant.
  • B) $(x-2)^2 + (-324)$ — vertex form; the constant $-324$ is exactly the minimum value.
  • C) $x(x-4) + (-320)$ — the $-320$ is the y-intercept, not the minimum.
  • D) $(x+16)(x-20)$ — factored form shows the zeros ($-16$ and $20$), not the minimum.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
